compaction_summary:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: The user wants to build a dedicated `/womb-wisdom-yoga-training` landing page as the highest-priority piece of a piecemeal WordPress migration for their client Chocolako. The Morocco YTT (August 1-22, 2026) needs this page ASAP because Chocolako has 4 months to fill the cohort. The page merges content from: (a) the existing chocolako.com/womb-wisdom-yoga-training page (18 sections), (b) Chocolako's PDF copy direction (Section 05, path 03), and (c) the warm editorial design system built in the previous session. The user also wants photos from Google Drive integrated into the page. The Next.js version serves as both a clickable demo and the design blueprint for WordPress migration. 2. Key Technical Concepts: - Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, TailwindCSS 4, Framer Motion, TypeScript - Warm editorial design: cacao (#482b17), espresso (#33281C), gold (#a67951), bone (#FAF8F5), sand (#EDE8E0), cream (#F5F1EB), rust (#8B5E3C) - Typography: Cormorant Garamond (font-serif) + Inter (font-sans) - Animation patterns: staggerContainer, staggerItem, fadeInUp from `@/lib/animations` - SectionWrapper from `@/components/shared/motion` for scroll-triggered sections - Subagent-driven development pattern for task execution - Piecemeal WordPress migration via WP-CLI + Kadence child theme (planned, not started) - HTML entities (`'`, `&`) must NOT be used in JavaScript string literals — only in JSX content between tags - Commit format: `(feat): description` — no co-author line, 2-3 line messages - Frontend-design skill loaded from `/Users/be.nvy/Downloads/SKILL.md` — editorial/magazine + luxury retreat aesthetic 3. Files and Code Sections: - `app/womb-wisdom-yoga-training/page.tsx` — Main route, server component with metadata. Renders all 17 YTT sections + StickyCTA + Footer. - `components/ytt/YTTHero.tsx` — Full-viewport hero with cacao warm overlay, "THE RETURN HOME" label, heading, date pill with backdrop blur, CTA button linking to #registration. - `components/ytt/YTTPainPoints.tsx` — 11 pain points with gold left borders on cream background. - `components/ytt/YTTFeminineBody.tsx` — 4 poetic lines as large italic serif on sand background. - `components/ytt/YTTWombCenter.tsx` — Short centered section with "Start Your Journey" CTA to #registration. - `components/ytt/YTTOverview.tsx` — Training description, white background. - `components/ytt/YTTFounder.tsx` — Split layout (photo placeholder left, bio right) with full credentials on cream background. - `components/ytt/YTTFaculty.tsx` — 6 guest experts in responsive 3-column grid on sand background. - `components/ytt/YTTModules.tsx` — 5 curriculum modules with gold numbering on bone background. - `components/ytt/YTTSchedule.tsx` — Daily timeline (7am-7pm) on white background. - `components/ytt/YTTVenue.tsx` — Bab Zouina details, 3 sacred commitments grid, amenity pills on sand background. - `components/ytt/YTTWhoIsFor.tsx` — 6 ideal candidate traits with gold diamond markers on cream background. **Fixed:** `'` in string literals changed to plain apostrophes. - `components/ytt/YTTGains.tsx` — 6 transformation outcomes with gold checkmarks on sand background. - `components/ytt/YTTTestimonials.tsx` — 3 testimonials (Librette, Dr. Micha T, Varda) with gold-border blockquotes. **Fixed:** `'` and `&` in string literals changed to plain characters. - `components/ytt/YTTPricing.tsx` — 3 room tiers ($7,888/$7,288/$6,688 standard rates) with Pay in Full + Pay Deposit buttons. - `components/ytt/YTTInclusions.tsx` — Two-column included/not-included lists. **Fixed:** removed `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, replaced `&` with `&` in string literals. - `components/ytt/YTTRegistration.tsx` — 3-step process cards, CTA, travel + certification requirements. Has `id="registration"` scroll anchor. **Fixed:** `'` in string literal. - `components/ytt/YTTStickyCTA.tsx` — Fixed bottom bar using useScroll/useMotionValueEvent, appears after scrolling past hero. - `components/ytt/YTTSchedule.tsx` — **Fixed:** `&` and `—` in string literals changed to plain `&` and `—`. - `components/shared/Navigation.tsx` — Updated Morocco link from `/womb-wisdom-morocco` to `/womb-wisdom-yoga-training`. - `components/sections/Pathways.tsx` — Added `href?: string` to PathCard interface, set path 03 href to `/womb-wisdom-yoga-training`, updated `` to use `path.href || "#"`. - `docs/plans/2026-04-12-womb-wisdom-ytt-design.md` — Full design document with 17 sections, photo asset table, WordPress migration notes. - `docs/plans/2026-04-12-womb-wisdom-ytt-implementation.md` — 10-task implementation plan with complete code for all components. - `public/images/ytt/` — Downloaded photos: - `morocco-hero.jpg` (296KB, 1620x1080) — Chocolako on Morocco sand dunes in white dress/turban - `founder.jpg` (244KB, 724x1086) — Chocolako in colorful outfit, dance pose, dark background - `founder-alt.jpg` (5.8MB, 2813x5000) — Chocolako in purple yoga outfit, jumping - `closing-1.jpg` (12MB, 4251x3401) — Large photo, needs optimization - `sfi-immersions.jpg` (53KB, 508x339) — Small group photo - `sfi-pathways.jpg` (1.2MB, 1350x900) — Pathways/SFI photo - `additional-1.heic` (2.3MB) — HEIF format, not JPEG - `additional-2.jpg` (124KB, 1086x724) — Women in meditation/prayer circle - `additional-3.jpg` (28KB, 339x508) — Small portrait 4. Errors and fixes: - **Commit format rejection:** User rejected first commit with co-author line and long message. User said: "no coauthor, 2-3 line commit msg, '(feat):...' formatting". Fixed by removing co-author line and using short `(feat): description` format for all subsequent commits. - **Stale TS diagnostics:** Every task triggered false "declared but its value is never read" diagnostics for newly added imports. These were always stale — builds passed clean. Ignored throughout. - **HTML entities in JS string literals:** `'` rendered as literal text "You thrive in women's community" instead of "You thrive in women's community". Root cause: HTML entities only work in JSX content between tags, not in JavaScript string constants. Fixed by replacing with plain characters (`'`, `&`, `—`) in YTTWhoIsFor, YTTTestimonials, YTTRegistration, YTTInclusions, YTTSchedule. - **dangerouslySetInnerHTML in YTTInclusions:** The subagent used `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` to work around the entity issue. Fixed by removing it and using plain characters in strings instead. Initial fix broke syntax (``) — fixed to just `{item}`. - **HEIF image format:** additional-1.jpg was actually HEIF format. Renamed to additional-1.heic. - **Large image files:** closing-1.jpg (12MB) and founder-alt.jpg (5.8MB) are very large for web use — need optimization before deployment. - **poppler installation:** PDF reading initially failed due to Xcode license not accepted. User accepted the license, poppler installed, but the Read tool still didn't detect it. Worked around by using PyMuPDF (fitz) for PDF text/link extraction instead. 5. Problem Solving: - Successfully extracted all content from both the existing chocolako.com page (via WebFetch) and the PDF (via PyMuPDF) to merge into the new design - Managed 8 subagent dispatches + 2 direct edits for 10 tasks total - All entity rendering issues identified and fixed across 5 files - Photos successfully downloaded from Google Drive direct download URLs - WordPress migration strategy documented but not yet started (waiting for admin + SSH access) 6. All user messages: - "Ok so now. recall the previous checkpoint and then we can talk about what's next" - "Not sure if we've done anything regarding this part of her site yet, but we're probably going to do piecemeal migration and this part of the site is most important https://chocolako.com/womb-wisdom-yoga-training/ Cause she has 4 months to close more people for the training in Morocco. Also inside /Users/be.nvy/Downloads/Chocolako_Homepage_FULL_2026.docx.pdf there was a bunch of links that link to photos. So, let's focus all our energy on that part of the site right now. It's also referenced in the doc" - "I installed poplar" (poppler) - "Or accepted the license I mean" - "Well part of the PDF has what she wants out of that part of the page. The design that just happened in the previous session was done based on her direction. Would like to see it integrated into current design but incorporate all the elements of the existing page just modernized to the design and also what's in the PDF. That make sense?" - "yeah, cause I plan on doing a piecemeal migration from the next.js state to her WordPress. If you want extra context before starting [shared two Google Doc URLs and detailed WordPress migration markdown docs]" - "Yeah use the current pricing. We can change as needed as she says" - "Yeah, let's do that" (approving Approach A) - "Yeah, let's do that, and maybe load the frontend skill before we do the design doc and move to planning" - [Invoked /superpowers:using-superpowers] - "/Users/be.nvy/Downloads/SKILL.md there" (frontend-design skill location) - "Yeah, let's do that for now" (approving design) - "1" (choosing subagent-driven development) - Rejected commit format: "no coauthor, 2-3 line commit msg, '(feat):...' formatting" - "rendering issue in the last point. also can we add the photos?" (with screenshot showing ' rendering bug) 7. Pending Tasks: - Commit the entity fixes (YTTWhoIsFor, YTTTestimonials, YTTRegistration, YTTInclusions, YTTSchedule) - Integrate downloaded photos into the YTT components (replace placeholder divs with actual `` or Next.js `` tags) - Optimize large images (closing-1.jpg at 12MB, founder-alt.jpg at 5.8MB) - Handle HEIC format file (additional-1.heic — may need conversion) - Commit photos and component updates 8. Current Work: I was fixing the `'` rendering bug the user reported (now fixed across 5 files but not yet committed) and downloading photos from Google Drive. I successfully downloaded 9 photos to `public/images/ytt/` and viewed them to understand their content: - morocco-hero.jpg → perfect for YTTHero background (Chocolako on sand dunes) - founder.jpg or founder-alt.jpg → for YTTFounder section - additional-2.jpg → women in ceremony, good for venue or community sections - closing-1.jpg → needs optimization, could work for a section break The entity fixes are done but not committed. The photos are downloaded but not yet integrated into components or committed. Some photos need optimization (12MB and 5.8MB are too large for web). 9. Optional Next Step: Integrate the downloaded photos into the YTT components — replace the placeholder `
` elements with actual images using Next.js `` component or `` tags. Key mappings: - `morocco-hero.jpg` → YTTHero.tsx (replace the sand-colored placeholder background) - `founder.jpg` or `founder-alt.jpg` → YTTFounder.tsx (replace the aspect-[3/4] placeholder) - `additional-2.jpg` → could work for YTTVenue.tsx or another section Then commit both the entity fixes and the photo integration together. The user said: "also can we add the photos?" — this is the immediate next task. If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/be.nvy/.claude/projects/-Users-be-nvy-chocolako-site/b93707a6-9bf1-439e-bf95-104c12d15663.jsonl Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.
